problem_statement: Code evaluation for LLM-generated code ("vibe coding") is anchored to functional correctness (pass@k) and overlooks the non-functional instructions and preferences users routinely screen for, so models can score high on benchmarks yet fail the human "vibe check."
magnitude_evidence: LMArena coding subset "contains over 800K human votes" aggregated into Elo ratings; 31 LLMs across 10 families evaluated with "over 10K instruction-level evaluations."
insufficiency_evidence: "rankings of code LLMs from Copilot Arena exhibit weak or negative correlations with functional scores on popular benchmarks"; even the best model reaches only 46.75% and 40.95% task-level success under five instructions.
solution_mechanisms: VeriCode taxonomy of 30 verifiable code instructions
deterministic linter/AST/regex verifiers returning binary pass/fail
SWE-IF testbed augmenting BigCodeBench and LiveCodeBench
composite functional + instruction-following score as human-preference proxy
verifiable reward signal for model training/RLVR
notes: Header title "Vibe checker" differs from the paper's actual title "SWE-IF: Aligning Code Evaluation with Human Preference"; some math notation is garbled in extraction but content is intact.
